reasoning: The current value of OVX (crude oil volatility) is around 35.72. The historical median for OVX is generally lower, around 25-30, meaning it currently sits somewhat elevated following recent spikes in April and May 2025, but is trending downward. Volatility usually exhibits strong mean reversion back to the lower baseline, interspersed with occasional right-skewed spikes. Because we are currently above the long-term median, the probability of remaining or increasing above 35.72 in the near term is slightly less than 50% (the FRED trend analysis estimates ~40% for the near-term). I've set the shortest horizon (June 15) to 0.40. Following instructions that uncertainty should increase with horizon (probabilities migrating toward 0.5), I gradually raise the probabilities for the later dates. Over longer horizons, there is more opportunity for new geopolitical or supply/demand shocks to cause fresh volatility spikes, which naturally pulls the long-term estimate closer to a neutral 50% given the right-skewed nature of volatility indices.
